name: tools
context: Define and expose executable MCP tools — functions that AI models discover via tools/list and invoke via tools/call
info: MCP tools are model-controlled executable functions that servers expose to AI applications. Tools let agents perform actions: query databases, call APIs, manipulate files, run computations. Each tool has a name, description (used by the LLM for selection), and an inputSchema (JSON Schema defining parameters). Tools are discovered via `tools/list` and invoked via `tools/call`. Tool results return content arrays (text, images, audio, resource links) with an optional `isError` flag. Tools may also define an outputSchema for structured responses. The server declares the `tools` capability during initialization, optionally with `listChanged: true` to notify clients when tools are added or removed at runtime.
triggers: add a tool to my MCP server
create an MCP tool with input schema

sections.title: Tool definition schema
sections.body: Each tool requires `name` (unique identifier), `description` (used by LLMs for selection), and `inputSchema` (JSON Schema for parameters). Optional fields: `title` (display name), `outputSchema` (structured result validation), and `annotations` (behavior hints). In Python FastMCP, these are auto-generated from type hints and docstrings. In TypeScript, use Zod schemas passed to `server.tool()`.

sections.title: Tool discovery via tools/list
sections.body: Clients discover available tools by sending a `tools/list` request. The response contains a `tools` array with each tool's name, description, and inputSchema. Supports pagination via `cursor`/`nextCursor`. Servers that declare `listChanged: true` in capabilities will send `notifications/tools/list_changed` when tools are added, removed, or modified, prompting clients to re-fetch.

sections.title: Tool invocation via tools/call
sections.body: Clients invoke tools by sending a `tools/call` request with `name` and `arguments`. The server validates arguments against the inputSchema, executes the tool, and returns a result with a `content` array. Content items can be text, images, audio, resource links, or embedded resources. Set `isError: true` for execution failures. Use `structuredContent` alongside `content` for machine-readable results when an `outputSchema` is defined.

sections.title: Error handling patterns
sections.body: Two error mechanisms: (1) **Protocol errors** use JSON-RPC error responses for invalid tool names (`-32602`) or server errors (`-32603`). (2) **Execution errors** return a normal result with `isError: true` and an error message in the content array. Protocol errors mean the request was malformed; execution errors mean the tool ran but failed. Always return meaningful error messages to help the LLM recover.

sections.title: Tool annotations
sections.body: Tool annotations provide optional metadata about tool behavior. Clients MUST treat annotations as untrusted unless from trusted servers. Annotations help clients make decisions about confirmation prompts, display, and safety. Content items also support annotations with `audience` (user/assistant), `priority` (0.0-1.0), and `lastModified` (ISO 8601 timestamp).
